description: Rust-first, Leptos-native web framework and design constitution for replacing vibe-coded websites and web apps with a strict, typographic, grid-governed, Crouwelian alternative. V0 proves one grammar across editorial and SaaS surfaces, ships a CLI and agent-readable contract, and stays narrow, severe, adaptable, and constrained rather than broad or theme-driven.

userStories.title: Freeze the constitution and naming model
userStories.description: As the New Alphabet team, we need a fixed doctrine, naming system, and anti-vibe-coded position so the framework is governed by law instead of local taste.
acceptanceCriteria: The core thesis is explicit: New Alphabet is a Crouwelian design constitution for the web and a strict alternative to vibe-coded products

Core philosophy is written and stable: order before ornament, grid as operating law, information over personality, constraint generates freedom, systems over pages, medium truth, and agents need rules not vibes

The aesthetic position is explicit: modern, severe, typographic, structured, calm, exact, and recognizable, while rejecting nostalgic, ornamental, cute, trend-chasing, or generic contemporary SaaS output
Goals, non-goals, and anti-patterns are documented clearly enough to narrow implementation choices
Naming rules for foundations, primitives, components, and recipes are fixed and role-based rather than decorative
The doctrine is primary and the framework, CLI, and agent contract are defined as downstream expressions of that doctrine
Documentation for this story is exact, restrained, and systemic rather than promotional
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Ports canonical backlog item B1 and milestone M0. This story owns the core thesis, aesthetic position, goals, non-goals, anti-pattern posture, naming rules, and doctrine hierarchy from the prior PRD.

userStories.title: Implement the foundation runtime
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the constitutional rules to exist as real runtime law so spacing, type, density, color roles, borders, motion, state, and breakpoints are not left to improvisation.
acceptanceCriteria: A foundation layer exists in Rust with explicit contracts for grid, spacing scale, type scale, density modes, color roles, border rules, motion rules, breakpoints, and state tokens
The grid defines named page and region behavior rather than ad hoc layout choices
Spacing is finite and token-driven, and arbitrary spacing values are structurally disallowed in framework code
Typography supports both editorial reading and dense operational surfaces under one grammar
Density modes are defined and visibly alter spacing, type, and dense data behavior
Color roles are semantic and do not create open-ended palette freedom at V0
Foundation rules are documented well enough to support primitive and recipe implementation
The implementation is SSR-safe and ready for hydration
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Ports canonical backlog item B2, milestone M1 foundations scope, and the full design-system foundation contracts from the prior PRD.

userStories.title: Implement structural primitives
userStories.description: As a product engineer, I need structural primitives that define constitutional page geometry so layouts are composed from law rather than invented per screen.
acceptanceCriteria: AppShell, PageGrid, Region, Rail, Stack, Row, ColumnGroup, Panel, Band, SectionHeader, and Divider exist as real Leptos primitives
Primitive APIs are narrow, structural, and aligned with the grid and spacing law
Primitives support predictable region placement, rail behavior, stacking rhythm, and bounded panel structure
Primitives remain structural and do not take on domain-specific semantic meaning
Primitive composition is responsive, SSR-safe, and hydration-safe
Allowed and disallowed region combinations are explicit enough to support later validation
Primitive examples exist and prove the geometry can serve both editorial and SaaS surfaces
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Ports canonical backlog item B3 and the primitive contracts for AppShell, PageGrid, Region, Rail, Stack, and Panel from the prior PRD.

userStories.title: Implement the core semantic component set
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the minimum semantic component layer so serious product UI can be built without broad component-library sprawl or visual wrapper abstractions.
acceptanceCriteria: A minimum semantic component inventory exists, including Button, LinkAction, TextField, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, RadioGroup, Switch, StatusBadge, InlineAlert, EmptyState, Table, MetricBlock, NavIndex, CommandBar, FilterRail, DetailPane, and Pagination
Components expose finite, role-based APIs rather than open-ended visual props
State coverage is explicit where relevant, including default, loading, empty, error, success, disabled, focus-visible, hover, and active behavior
Dense surfaces such as Table and DetailPane define truncation, wrapping, loading, unavailable, and zero-result behavior
Accessibility requirements are first-class, including semantic HTML, keyboard use, accessible names, and color-independent meaning
Components remain built on primitives and do not bypass the foundation layer
Component examples and state examples exist and are documented
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Ports canonical backlog item B4, milestone M2, the state model, and the component contracts for Button, TextField, Select, StatusBadge, InlineAlert, EmptyState, Table, CommandBar, and DetailPane from the prior PRD.

ArticleShell keeps the reading flow primary while still supporting adjacent navigation and metadata

DocsShell proves that navigation and reading flow can coexist under the same grammar
Editorial recipes define required and optional regions, adaptation limits, and responsive behavior
The editorial surfaces visibly belong to the same family as later SaaS recipes without looking identical

userStories.title: Implement the CLI surface

acceptanceCriteria: The CLI name is `new-alphabet` and it supports at minimum init, add recipe, add component, explain, validate, and export context

acceptanceCriteria: SettingsWorkspace exists with section navigation and editable content as required regions
DashboardShell exists and proves summary and dense-data blocks can remain within the same grammar
Optional context help and status feedback are supported without weakening structure
The implementation is rooted in the documentation sections on SaaS use cases, recipe contracts, and initial inventory
Examples prove these surfaces remain part of the same family as editorial recipes
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Rooted in documentation sections Use Cases, Initial Inventory, and the SettingsWorkspace contract plus DashboardShell inventory preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec.

userStories.title: Export canonical schemas and reference examples
userStories.description: As an AI-assisted builder, I need exported schemas and examples so the system can be loaded into a coding session without reading the entire repo by hand.
acceptanceCriteria: Exported artifacts cover foundations, primitives, components, recipes, anti-patterns, and prompt-intent examples
Reference examples are tied to the exported schema shape rather than stored as disconnected prose
The export set is rooted in the documentation sections on product shape, installation story, and agent model
Exports are versioned and deterministic
The output is sufficient to support prompt-to-plan and prompt-to-scaffold workflows
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Rooted in documentation sections Product Shape, Installation Story, Agent Model, and the agent contract recommended artifacts preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec.

userStories.title: Implement validation rules and anti-pattern reporting
userStories.description: As a maintainer or coding agent, I need real constitutional validation so drift is identified as rules violations rather than style opinions.
acceptanceCriteria: Validation rules cover composition, spacing, state coverage, naming, accessibility, and anti-pattern usage
Anti-pattern reporting exists for visual, layout, API, implementation, and agentic drift
Validation messages are rooted in the documentation sections on anti-vibe-coded position, design rules, acceptance gates, and risks
Reports distinguish errors, warnings, and notes and can suggest repairs in framework terms
Validation tests prove the system catches meaningful drift rather than superficial style differences
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Rooted in documentation sections Anti-Vibe-Coded Position, Design Rules, Acceptance Gates, Risks, and the validation categories and anti-pattern registry preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec.

userStories.title: Implement CLI init and add scaffolding flows
userStories.description: As a developer, I need initialization and scaffold commands so new projects and new structures start inside the doctrine instead of inventing their own system.
acceptanceCriteria: `new-alphabet init` creates or wires the minimum project structure needed for New Alphabet
`new-alphabet add recipe <name>` and `new-alphabet add component <name>` scaffold approved structures only
Scaffold output binds to foundations, primitives, state coverage, and documentation expectations
The implementation is rooted in the documentation sections on installation story, CLI surface, and the command contracts preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec
Write behavior is explicit and does not rewrite unrelated project structure
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful
A single local commit is created for this story, then pushed to remote, with a technically accurate commit message that includes the story ID and a random Crouwel quote or theme
userStories.notes: Rooted in documentation sections Installation Story, CLI Surface, and the init/add command contracts preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec.

userStories.title: Implement CLI explain, inspect, validate, export, and plan flows
userStories.description: As a developer or coding agent, I need the read-heavy CLI surfaces so New Alphabet can explain itself, inspect real projects, validate drift, export context, and plan from intent in framework terms.
acceptanceCriteria: `new-alphabet explain`, `inspect`, `validate`, and `export context` exist and operate in framework language
`new-alphabet plan <intent>` exists if shipped and emits structured implementation plans rather than design prose
Inspect output maps code back to recipes, primitives, components, and rule violations
Export context produces the canonical bundle needed for coding-session use

userStories.title: Publish the exact remote-ready V0 repository
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I need the remote repository to contain exactly the complete V0 New Alphabet offering so a user or coding agent can clone it and find the doctrine, runtime, demos, CLI, contract surface, docs, and release posture in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: The remote contains the framework crates, CLI, schema layer, agent layer, docs app, demo-blog app, demo-saas app, examples, prompts, and scripts described in the documentation and canonical spec
The remote contains the public documentation, contributor guidance, roadmap, release notes template, issue templates, and license needed for OSS launch
The remote carries the demos, exported context support, validation system, and prompt materials needed to prove the agent thesis
The remote reflects the doctrine, milestones, acceptance gates, and definition of done without hidden missing pieces
All prior user stories required for V0 are represented in the remote in a form that another developer or coding agent can actually use
This story closes only when the remote has exactly what the documentation and canonical spec say V0 must ship, with no critical omissions
Any code written for this story remains terse, constrained, and purposeful

userStories.notes: Rooted in documentation sections V0 Scope, Definition of Done, Product Shape, Monorepo Shape, Risks, and the public-OSS posture preserved in prd_v1 canonicalSpec. This is the explicit remote-completeness story.
